name: Strengthening Rural Communities (SRC)
provider: Foundation For Rural And Regional Renewal
requirements_summary: FRRR supports not-for-profit organisations that are incorporated or have an ABN. Projects must have a charitable purpose and provide broad community benefit. For-profit organisations and individuals are not eligible.

name: First Nations Targeted Grant
Indigenous Advancement Strategy (IAS)

grant_structure.duration_note: Varies per Grant Opportunity Guidelines. Typically 1-5 years.

requirements_summary: The Indigenous Advancement Strategy (IAS) funds programs benefiting First Nations Australians across six key components: Jobs and Economy, Children and Schooling, Safety and Wellbeing, Culture and Capability, Remote Australian Strategies, and Research and Evaluation. The Australian Government allocated $7.2 billion to IAS over four years to 2026-27. Applicants should check GrantConnect for current funding opportunities or contact their local NIAA Regional Office. Communities can also submit Community Initiated Proposals directly to NIAA. Funded providers must work in genuine partnership with First Nations communities in project design and delivery.

SEDI First Nations Social Enterprise Grants
provider: Impact Investing Australia + Malu Pty Ltd (admin) — Dept of Social Services (funder)

priority_cohorts: Trading Indigenous owned or controlled organisations

requirements_summary: Eligible applicants must be Australian social enterprises or trading Indigenous organisations with annual trading revenue exceeding $50,000, currently providing direct benefits to people experiencing entrenched disadvantage, and seeking to scale impact in Department of Social Services outcome areas. New applicants submit an Expression of Interest for a preliminary eligibility phone conversation with IIA, then submit full applications on a rolling basis. Grants up to $120,000 fund capability building services (financial, legal, consulting, impact measurement). The program operates on rolling rounds through early 2027, with no set application deadline.

requirements_summary: Grants are available to First Nations-led organisations in Tasmania, the Northern Territory, the Torres Strait, regional and remote Queensland, and regional and remote South Australia to support self-determination.

name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander(ATSI) initiatives
SEDI Capability Building Grant
provider: Impact Investing Australia (admin) — Dept of Social Services (funder)

priority_cohorts: Organisations serving communities, employment, education and First Nations Australians

requirements_summary: Eligible applicants must be social enterprises or trading Indigenous organisations with defined social benefit, trading revenue exceeding $50,000 annually, providing direct services to people experiencing entrenched disadvantage in approved DSS outcome areas across Australia. Grants up to $120,000 support capability building services including financial advice, impact measurement, business consulting and legal support. This is a competitive process with no guaranteed award for meeting eligibility criteria. Applications can register interest with Impact Investing Australia for updates on extension opportunities.

name: IBA Start-Up Finance Package
Supply Nation Certification + Buyer Access
requirements_summary: The program is open to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander businesses, aiming to connect them with corporate, government, and not-for-profit members. Verified Indigenous businesses can join to access procurement opportunities and grow their businesses.

Indigenous Cultural Preservation Grant

priority_cohorts: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Australians

requirements_summary: Eligible organisations must be Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander Corporations, government entities, companies, associations, not-for-profit organisations, or local government councils with an ABN, GST registration (if applicable), and an Australian financial institution account. Projects must be located in Australian states and territories (ACT, NSW, VIC, SA, WA, QLD, NT, TAS) and commence after 1 July 2026. Grant funding ranges from $20,000 to $200,000 for projects lasting 12 to 36 months. Applications must be submitted through SmartyGrants by 16 March 2026 at 11:30 pm ACT Local Time. Late submissions will not be accepted.

name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Arts Development Fund
requirements_summary: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander individuals, groups, and organisations based in Queensland can apply for one-off grants to support new creative performing arts work (up to $70,000) or visual arts market development activities (up to $30,000) for projects up to one year. Applicants must be Australian citizens/permanent residents (if individual), have an active ABN and bank account, not owe Arts Queensland money, not have received funding in the previous round, and submit applications by 2pm on 22 June 2026. Additional funding up to $10,000 is available for disability access costs.
